info.title: Lists
info.x-hubspot-api-use-case: You want to bulk add contacts to a particular list, or remove company records from a company list.
info.x-hubspot-introduction: Use the lists API to manage list memberships for object lists.

put.description: Update the name of a list. The name must be globally unique relative to all other public lists in the portal.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the list to update

parameters.name: listName
parameters.description: The name to update the list to

parameters.name: includeFilters
parameters.description: A flag indicating whether or not the response object list definition should include a filter branch definition. By default, object list definitions will not have their filter branch definitions included in the response

put.summary: Add and/or Remove Records from a List
put.description:
Add and/or remove records that have already been created in the system to and/or from a list.

This endpoint only works for lists that have a _processingType_ of _MANUAL_ or _SNAPSHOT_.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the _MANUAL_ or _SNAPSHOT_ list

get.description: Fetch a single list by **ILS list ID**.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the list to fetch

parameters.name: includeFilters
parameters.description: A flag indicating whether or not the response object list definition should include a filter branch definition. By default, object list definitions will not have their filter branch definitions included in the response

delete.description: Delete a list by **ILS list ID**. Lists deleted through this endpoint can be restored up to 90-days following the delete. After 90-days, the list is purged and can no longer be restored.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the list to delete

put.description: Update the filter branch definition of a _DYNAMIC_ list. Once updated, the list memberships will be re-evaluated and updated to match the new definition.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the list to update

parameters.name: enrollObjectsInWorkflows
parameters.description: A flag indicating whether or not the memberships added to the list as a result of the filter change should be enrolled in workflows that are relevant to this list

post.description: Search lists by list name or page through all lists by providing an empty _query_ value.

put.summary: Moves a list to a given folder
put.description: Given a list and a folder, the list will be moved to that folder.

Add the records provided to the list. Records that do not exist or that are already members of the list are ignored.

This endpoint only works for lists that have a _processingType_ of _MANUAL_ or _SNAPSHOT_.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the _MANUAL_ or _SNAPSHOT_ list

get.description: Fetch a single list by list name and object type.

parameters.name: listName
parameters.description: The name of the list to fetch. This is **not** case sensitive

parameters.name: objectTypeId
parameters.description: The object type ID of the object types stored by the list to fetch. For example, _0-1_ for a _CONTACT_ list

parameters.name: includeFilters
parameters.description: A flag indicating whether or not the response object list definition should include a filter branch definition. By default, object list definitions will not have their filter branch definitions included in the response

put.description: This moves the folder from its current location to a new location. It updates the parent of this folder to the new Id given.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder to be moved.

parameters.name: newParentFolderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the destination parent folder.

get.summary: Translate legacy list ID to modern ID
get.description: This API allows translation of legacy list id to list id. This is a temporary API allowed for mapping old id's to new id's and will expire on May 30th, 2025.

parameters.name: legacyListId
parameters.description: The legacy list id from lists v1 API

post.description: This API allows translation of a batch of legacy list id's to list id's. This allows for a maximum of 10,000 id's. This is a temporary API allowed for mapping old id's to new id's and will expire on May 30th, 2025.

put.description: Restore a previously deleted list by **ILS list ID**. Deleted lists are eligible to be restored up-to 90-days after the list has been deleted.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the list to restore

put.description: Renames the given folderId with a new name.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder to be renamed.

parameters.name: newFolderName
parameters.description: The new name to assign to the folder.

get.description:
Fetch the memberships of a list in order sorted by the time the records were added to the list.

The _recordId_s are sorted in *ascending* order if an _after_ offset or no offset is provided. If only a _before_ offset is provided, then the records are sorted in *descending* order.

The _after_ offset parameter will take precedence over the _before_ offset in a case where both are provided.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the list

parameters.name: after
parameters.description:
The paging offset token for the page that comes _after_ the previously requested records.

If provided, then the records in the response will be the records following the offset, sorted in *ascending* order. Takes precedence over the _before_ offset

parameters.name: before
parameters.description:
The paging offset token for the page that comes _before_ the previously requested records.

If provided, then the records in the response will be the records preceding the offset, sorted in *descending* order

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of records to return in the response. The maximum _limit_ is 250

put.summary: Add all records from source list
put.description:
Add all of the records from a *source list* (specified by the _sourceListId_) to a *destination list* (specified by the _listId_). Records that are already members of the *destination list* will be ignored. The *destination* and *source list* IDs must be different. The *destination* and *source lists* must contain records of the same type (e.g. contacts, companies, etc.).

This endpoint only works for *destination lists* that have a _processingType_ of _MANUAL_ or _SNAPSHOT_. The *source list* can have any _processingType_.

This endpoint only supports a _sourceListId_ for lists with less than 100,000 memberships.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the _MANUAL_ or _SNAPSHOT_ *destination list*, which the *source list* records are added to

parameters.name: sourceListId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the *source list* to grab the records from, which are then added to the *destination list*

get.summary: Get lists record is member of
get.description: For given record provide lists this record is member of.

parameters.name: objectTypeId
parameters.description: Object type id of the record

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Id of the record

get.description: Fetch multiple lists in a single request by **ILS list ID**. The response will include the definitions of all lists that exist for the _listIds_ provided.

parameters.name: listIds
parameters.description: The **ILS IDs** of the lists to fetch

parameters.name: includeFilters
parameters.description: A flag indicating whether or not the response object list definitions should include a filter branch definition. By default, object list definitions will not have their filter branch definitions included in the response

post.description: Create a new list with the provided object list definition.

get.description: Retrieves a folder and recursively includes all folders via the childNodes attribute.  The child lists field will be empty in all child nodes. Only the folder retrieved will include the child lists in that folder.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The Id of the folder to retrieve

post.description: Creates a folder with the given information.

delete.description: Deletes the folder with the given Id.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder to be deleted.

get.summary: Fetch List Memberships Ordered by ID
get.description:
Fetch the memberships of a list in order sorted by the _recordId_ of the records in the list.

The _recordId_s are sorted in *ascending* order if an _after_ offset or no offset is provided. If only a _before_ offset is provided, then the records are sorted in *descending* order.

The _after_ offset parameter will take precedence over the _before_ offset in a case where both are provided.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the list

parameters.name: after
parameters.description:
The paging offset token for the page that comes _after_ the previously requested records.

If provided, then the records in the response will be the records following the offset, sorted in *ascending* order. Takes precedence over the _before_ offset

parameters.name: before
parameters.description:
The paging offset token for the page that comes _before_ the previously requested records.

If provided, then the records in the response will be the records preceding the offset, sorted in *descending* order

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of records to return in the response. The maximum _limit_ is 250

delete.summary: Delete All Records from a List
delete.description:
Remove **all** of the records from a list. ***Note:*** *The list is not deleted.*

This endpoint only works for lists that have a _processingType_ of _MANUAL_ or _SNAPSHOT_.

This endpoint only supports lists that have less than 100,000 memberships.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the _MANUAL_ or _SNAPSHOT_ list

put.description:
Remove the records provided from the list. Records that do not exist or that are not members of the list are ignored.

This endpoint only works for lists that have a _processingType_ of _MANUAL_ or _SNAPSHOT_.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The **ILS ID** of the _MANUAL_ or _SNAPSHOT_ list

includeObjectsWithNoValueSet.description: Whether to include records where the property has no value.

value.description: The numeric value to compare against the property.

operator.description: The comparison operator applied to the numeric property value.

oneOf.description: A filter branch that combines conditions using OR logic.
A filter branch that combines conditions using AND logic.

PublicYearReference.title: YEAR

hour.description: The hour component of the year reference time.

month.description: The month component of the year reference date.

millisecond.description: The millisecond component of the year reference time.

referenceType.description: Identifies the reference type; always set to 'YEAR'.

day.description: The day component of the year reference date.

minute.description: The minute component of the year reference time.

second.description: The second component of the year reference time.

PublicYearReference.description: A date/time reference anchored to a specific year, with optional time components.

id.description: The unique identifier of the target list.

inListType.description: The type classification of the in-list filter condition.

PublicInListFilterMetadata.description: Metadata describing a filter condition that checks membership in a list.

ListFolderFetchResponse.description: Response containing a single retrieved list folder object.

PublicRangedDatePropertyOperation.title: RANGED_DATE

includeObjectsWithNoValueSet.description: Whether to include objects with no value set for this property

upperBound.description: The upper bound of the date range as an integer value

requiresTimeZoneConversion.description: Whether the date value requires time zone conversion

operationType.description: The operation type identifier, always set to RANGED_DATE

lowerBound.description: The lower bound of the date range as an integer value

operator.description: The comparison operator applied to the ranged date property

PublicRangedDatePropertyOperation.description: A property operation that filters records where a date value falls within a specified range.

listIds.description:
The _listIds_ that will be used to filter results by _listId_. If values are provided, then the response will only include results that have a _listId_ in this array.

If no value is provided, or if an empty list is provided, then the results will not be filtered by _listId_

offset.description: Value used to paginate through lists. The _offset_ provided in the response can be used in the next request to fetch the next page of results. Defaults to _0_ if no offset is provided

query.description: The _query_ that will be used to search for lists by list name. If no _query_ is provided, then the results will include all lists

count.description: The number of lists to include in the response. Defaults to _20_ if no value is provided. The max _count_ is _500_

processingTypes.description:
The _processingTypes_ that will be used to filter results by _processingType_. If values are provided, then the response will only include results that have a _processingType_ in this array.

If no value is provided, or if an empty list is provided, then results will not be filtered by _processingType_.

Valid _processingTypes_ are: _MANUAL_, _SNAPSHOT_, or _DYNAMIC_

additionalProperties.description:
The property names of any additional list properties to include in the response. Properties that do not exist or that are empty for a particular list are not included in the response.

By default, all requests will fetch the following properties for each list: _hs_list_size_, _hs_last_record_added_at_, _hs_last_record_removed_at_, _hs_folder_name_, and _hs_list_reference_count_

sort.description: The field by which to sort the returned list results

ListSearchRequest.description: Request body for searching and filtering lists by name, ID, or processing type, with pagination and additional property support

total.description: The total number of lists that match the search criteria

offset.description: Value to be passed in a future request to paginate through list search results

lists.description: The lists that matched the search criteria

hasMore.description: Whether or not there are more results to page through

ListSearchResponse.description: Response object containing matched lists, pagination details, and total result count for a list search query

A filter branch that filters based on unified event criteria.
A filter branch that filters based on associated object property conditions.
A filter branch that filters based on object association criteria.

PublicPropertyAssociationFilterBranchFil.description: A filter branch node within a property association filter, accepting one of several supported branch types including OR, AND, NOT ALL, NOT ANY, restricted, unified events, property association, or association branches.

subCategory.description: A specific category that contains more specific detail about the error

correlationId.description: A unique identifier for the request. Include this value with any error reports or support tickets

links.description: A map of link names to associated URIs containing documentation about the error or recommended remediation steps

message.description: A human readable message describing the error along with remediation steps where appropriate
message.example: Invalid input (details will vary based on the error)

example.message: Invalid input (details will vary based on the error)

Error.description: Represents a standardized API error response containing a message, error category, correlation ID for tracing, optional sub-category, contextual details, remediation links, and a list of granular error details.

listId.description: The V3 list id for the list

legacyListId.description: The legacy list id for the list

PublicMigrationMapping.description: Maps a legacy list ID to its corresponding V3 list ID to support list migration.

oneOf.description: A filter branch that combines association filter conditions using OR logic.
A filter branch applying AND logic to associated object conditions.
A filter branch excluding objects matching all specified conditions.
A filter branch excluding objects matching any specified conditions.
A restricted filter branch for association-scoped filtering.
A filter branch targeting unified event data for associations.
A filter branch filtering associations by property values.
A nested association filter branch for related object filtering.

PublicAssociationFilterBranchFilterBranc.description: A filter branch node within an association filter, accepting one of several supported branch types including OR, AND, NOT ALL, NOT ANY, restricted, unified events, property association, or association branches.

oneOf.description: A nested filter branch applying OR logic to its conditions.
A nested filter branch applying AND logic to its conditions.
A nested filter branch excluding objects matching all conditions.
A nested filter branch excluding objects matching any condition.
A nested restricted filter branch limiting filterable properties.
A nested filter branch scoped to unified event data.
A nested filter branch filtering by associated object properties.
A nested filter branch scoped to associated object relationships.

PublicAndFilterBranchFilterBranches.description: The collection of nested filter branches combined using AND logic. Each branch must be one of the supported filter branch types.

objectTypeId.description: The object type ID of the type of objects that the list will store

processingType.description: The processing type of the list. One of: _SNAPSHOT_, _MANUAL_, or _DYNAMIC_

customProperties.description: The list of custom properties to tie to the list. Custom property name is the key, the value is the value

listFolderId.description: The ID of the folder that the list should be created in. If left blank, then the list will be created in the root of the list folder structure

name.description: The name of the list, which must be globally unique across all public lists in the portal

filterBranch.description: The root filter branch defining membership criteria for dynamic lists.

ListCreateRequest.description: Request body for creating a new list, including the object type, processing type, name, optional folder placement, custom properties, and filter criteria.

PublicBoolPropertyOperation.title: BOOL

includeObjectsWithNoValueSet.description: Whether to include objects where the property has no value set.

operationType.description: The operation type identifier, fixed as 'BOOL'.

value.description: The boolean value to evaluate against the property.

operator.description: The comparison operator applied to the boolean property.

PublicBoolPropertyOperation.description: A boolean property filter operation that evaluates a property against a true/false value using a specified operator.

PublicAllPropertyTypesOperation.title: ALL_PROPERTY

includeObjectsWithNoValueSet.description: Whether to include objects where the property has no value set.

operationType.description: The operation type identifier, fixed as 'ALL_PROPERTY'.

operator.description: The comparison operator applied across all property types.

PublicAllPropertyTypesOperation.description: Defines a filter operation applicable to all property types, requiring an operator and specifying whether to include objects with no value set.

property.description: The name of the event property to filter on.

operation.description: The filter operation applied to the specified property, supporting multiple property types.

PublicEventFilterMetadata.description: Defines filter metadata for an event, pairing a property name with a typed filter operation to evaluate against that property.

PublicDatePoint.title: DATE

month.description: The month component of the date point (1–12).

hour.description: The hour component of the time (0–23).

year.description: The year component of the date point.

timezoneSource.description: The source used to determine the time zone.

millisecond.description: The millisecond component of the time (0–999).

timeType.description: The time type identifier, fixed as 'DATE'.

zoneId.description: The IANA time zone ID used to interpret the date point.

day.description: The day component of the date point (1–31).

minute.description: The minute component of the time (0–59).

second.description: The second component of the date-time value.

PublicDatePoint.description: Represents a specific date point with year, month, day, and optional time components, anchored to a time zone.

amount.description: The magnitude of the time offset.

offsetDirection.description: The direction of the time offset (e.g., forward or backward).

timeUnit.description: The unit of time for the offset (e.g., days, hours, minutes).

PublicTimeOffset.description: Defines a time offset with a magnitude, unit, and direction for relative time calculations.

link.description: A direct link to the request for the next page of records

after.description: The offset for the next page of records

NextPage.description: Pagination metadata containing a cursor and direct link to retrieve the next page of results.

PublicRollingPropertyUpdatedOperation.title: ROLLING_PROPERTY_UPDATED

includeObjectsWithNoValueSet.description: Whether to include records that have no value set for the property.

operationType.description: The operation type identifier, fixed as ROLLING_PROPERTY_UPDATED.

numberOfDays.description: The number of days in the rolling window for the property update check.

operator.description: The comparison operator applied in the rolling property updated operation.

PublicRollingPropertyUpdatedOperation.description: Defines a rolling filter operation that matches records whose property was updated within a specified number of days.
